brief_justification: Arrives at exact correct answer (No) with complete, valid dependency accounting; traces compile-time and runtime axes, identifies the bidirectional cycle, and provides remediation paths across multiple levels; proceeds linearly without naming biases or reflecting on its own method; uses before/after tables and a cycle diagram as implicit verification but no counterfactual or boundary-condition test; presents conclusions without structured uncertainty analysis or confidence calibration; mentions two structural remediation alternatives but does not evaluate competing interpretations of the main question; explicit tables allow full reproduction of the derivation chain; avoids treating all circular structures as invalid and correctly classifies this particular circularity as problematic, but does not explicitly name the anti-pattern from the gate; avoids most suppressed failure modes — no post hoc rationalization, no explanation/answer decoupling — but does not explicitly address any suppressed term; reasoning is linear tabular analysis rather than following the scan→classify→gate→convergence-check topology structure
